Indigenous Australians Civil Rights Movement← All Lessons
Lesson 1 of 4

White Australia Policy & The Stolen Generations

💭 Thinking Question

Imagine the government passed a law that said only certain types of people were welcome in Australia — everyone else would be forced to leave or change who they are. Who would be affected? How would people react today?

🎯 Learning Intention

By the end of this lesson I will be able to explain the purpose of the White Australia Policy and Assimilation Policy, describe who was affected and how, and analyse primary sources including personal accounts and infographics to understand the impact of the Stolen Generations.

⚠️ Warning: This lesson contains historical images, stories and film involving First Nations peoples, including images of deceased persons. Please engage with care and respect.

The White Australia Policy

The White Australia Policy refers to a collection of government policies introduced during the 1800s to mid-1900s to keep Australia mostly of European descent. Study the page from your booklet below:

The cartoons and quotes below show how the policy was presented to the public at the time:

FederationA group of states/countries joined under one government.
ImmigrationComing to live permanently in a foreign country.
RestrictionA limiting condition or legal measure.
DeportedExpelled from a country on grounds of illegal status.
ProhibitedForbidden; banned by law.
Dictation testWriting a passage aloud — used to exclude non-Europeans.

The questions page from your booklet is shown below for reference:

White Australia Policy Questions

1What was the aim of the 'White Australia Policy'? Was it successful?
2Who suffered because of this policy, and how?
3What was the purpose of the Immigration Restriction Act 1901?
4Why do you think the Government excluded certain groups from immigrating to Australia?
5How were people encouraged to immigrate to Australia? What groups were targeted?
📹 Watch
Ending the White Australia Policy (ABC Education)

The Before, During & After question guide from your booklet:

Ending the White Australia Policy — Before, During & After

1Before viewing: Think about your group of friends. How would you describe their cultural heritage? Are they all from the same place?
2aDuring viewing: Who do you think Al Grassby was referring to as being upset about the end of the White Australia policy?
2bWhy were so many Vietnamese people accepted into Australia after 1975? What impact did this have?
2cWhat were the immediate and long-term effects of the abolition of the White Australia policy?
3After viewing: Ian Macphee says in 1979–82 both parties agreed on accepting refugees. How are political parties different today? What caused the change?

The Assimilation Policy & The Stolen Generations

Study the Assimilation Policy page from your booklet — it shows the government's approach and the language used at the time:

Below are perspectives from Aboriginal Australians who lived through this policy, and the Perspectives Sorting Activity:

Perspectives Sorting Activity

Using the quotes from the booklet above, complete both boxes below.

Government aim — use quotes to explain why they created this policy
Impact on Aboriginal Australians — use quotes to show how people felt

The Stolen Generations

From the 1890s to the 1970s, the Australian Government used the law to remove First Nations children from their families. The historical photographs below are from your booklet:

The bar chart and newspaper advertisement below show the reasons recorded for removing Aboriginal girls, and how children were advertised for placement:

📹 Watch
The Stolen Generations
📹 Watch
Took the Children Away — Archie Roach

Australia's Stolen Generations Infographic

Study this infographic carefully — you will need it to answer the Infographic Questions below:

The Infographic Questions from your booklet:

Infographic Questions

1Who produced this infographic? What do they want you to understand, and how do they want you to feel?
2Why did the creators choose graphics over full sentences? Which style do you prefer? Explain.
3List three significant numbers from the infographic and explain what they mean:
Number 1:
Number 2:
Number 3:
4What is your emotional response to this infographic? Refer to at least one graph or graphic.
5Has the number of First Nations children in out-of-home care increased or decreased since the 1970s? What does that tell us?

The Stolen Generations Report task from your booklet:

Stolen Generations Report

After watching the videos and examining the sources, write a 1–2 paragraph report addressing all the dot points shown in your booklet above (Who, What, Where, When, Why, How, long-term impacts).

🪞 Lesson Reflection

What is the most important thing you learned in this lesson? Why does it matter?
How did learning about these policies and their effects make you feel? What questions do you still have?
Why is it important for all Australians — including non-Indigenous Australians — to understand this history?
